item.title: Sports News Summary — 2026-04-16
item.description: As the NFL draft approaches, teams are focusing on key prospects, while Tiger Woods faces legal challenges and major sports figures make headlines for various reasons.

item.title: Sports News Summary — 2026-04-15
item.description: The NBA play-in games concluded with the Warriors and 76ers advancing, while MLB celebrates Jackie Robinson's legacy amid executive changes in the Players Association.

item.title: Sports News Summary — 2026-04-14
item.description: LaMelo Ball's clutch performance led the Hornets to victory over the Heat in a dramatic play-in game, while the Blazers secured the 7th seed in the playoffs.

item.title: Sports News Summary — 2026-04-13
item.description: The 2026 WNBA draft saw Azzi Fudd selected first overall, while UCLA set a record with five first-round picks.

item.title: Sports News Summary — 2026-04-12
item.description: Rory McIlroy won his second consecutive Masters title, while Doc Rivers is leaving the Bucks and the NBA playoffs are set to begin.

item.title: Sports News Summary — 2026-04-11
item.description: Rory McIlroy's dramatic Saturday at the Masters sets up a competitive final round, while UFC and NCAA hockey also saw significant developments.

item.title: Sports News Summary — 2026-04-10
item.description: Rory McIlroy leads the Masters by six shots, while the Celtics tie an NBA record for three-pointers made in a game.

item.title: Sports News Summary — 2026-04-09
item.description: Rory McIlroy co-leads the Masters after a strong opening round, while NBA star Joel Embiid undergoes surgery for appendicitis.

item.title: Sports News Summary — 2026-04-08
item.description: The Oklahoma City Thunder secured the No. 1 seed in the NBA playoffs, while Scottie Scheffler and Bryson DeChambeau make headlines at the Masters.

item.title: Sports News Summary — 2026-04-07
item.description: The Colorado Avalanche clinched the Central Division title and the top seed in the Western Conference, while a brawl erupted in an MLB game between the Angels and Braves.

item.title: Sports News Summary — 2026-04-06
item.description: Michigan secured its second NCAA basketball title, defeating UConn, while the Spurs' Victor Wembanyama exited a game due to injury.

item.title: Sports News Summary — 2026-04-05
item.description: UCLA celebrates its first women's NCAA championship while Luka Doncic seeks treatment for his hamstring injury ahead of the NBA playoffs.

item.title: Sports News Summary — 2026-04-04
item.description: Michigan and UConn advance to the NCAA championship game, while Jo Adell's defensive heroics secure a win for the Angels.

item.title: Sports News Summary — 2026-04-03
item.description: Geno Auriemma criticized officiating after UConn's loss, while UCLA reached its first NCAA title game and Cooper Flagg became the youngest player with a 50-point NBA game.
